channel.title: Protein Design Podcast
channel.description: Daily automated digest of protein design, antibody engineering & enzyme design research
channel.itunes:author: Eva Dai
channel.itunes:summary: Daily automated digest of protein design, antibody engineering & enzyme design research

item.title: Daily Podcast 2026-03-10
item.description: Why are viral capsids almost always icosahedral? | The answer comes down to a fundamental constraint: viruses need to pack their genetic material efficiently inside a protective shell, but they're limited by how many different proteins they can encode. | Here's where geometry becomes crucial.
item.itunes:author: Eva Dai
item.itunes:summary: Why are viral capsids almost always icosahedral? | The answer comes down to a fundamental constraint: viruses need to pack their genetic material efficiently inside a protective shell, but they're limited by how many different proteins they can encode. | Here's where geometry becomes crucial.
